title: Getting Started with RankMap
text: RankMap R package fast, robust, scalable reference-based cell type annotation single-cell spatial transcriptomics data. works transforming gene expression matrices sparse ranked representations training multinomial logistic regression model using glmnet framework. rank-based approach improves robustness batch effects, platform differences, partial gene coverage—especially beneficial technologies Xenium MERFISH. RankMap supports commonly used data structures including Seurat, SingleCellExperiment, SpatialExperiment. workflow includes flexible preprocessing steps top-K gene masking, binning, expression weighting, scaling, followed efficient model training rapid prediction. Compared existing tools SingleR, RCTD (via spacexr), Azimuth, RankMap achieves comparable superior accuracy significantly faster runtime, making particularly well suited high-throughput applications large datasets. vignette provides quick-start guide using RankMap cell type prediction.

title: Getting Started with RankMap
text: Install RankMap GitHub:
code: devtools::install_github("jinming-cheng/RankMap")

title: Getting Started with RankMap
text: Load example single-cell RNA-seq dataset (17,597 genes x 150 cells): Load example Xenium spatial transcriptomics dataset (313 genes x 150 cells):
code: library(RankMap) seu_sc <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "seu_sc.rds", package = "RankMap")) seu_sc #> Loading required package: SeuratObject #> Loading required package: sp #> 'SeuratObject' was built under R 4.4.0 but the current version is #> 4.4.1; it is recomended that you reinstall 'SeuratObject' as the ABI #> for R may have changed #> 'SeuratObject' was built with package 'Matrix' 1.7.0 but the current #> version is 1.7.1; it is recomended that you reinstall 'SeuratObject' as #> the ABI for 'Matrix' may have changed #>  #> Attaching package: 'SeuratObject' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, t #> An object of class Seurat  #> 17597 features across 150 samples within 1 assay  #> Active assay: RNA (17597 features, 0 variable features) #>  2 layers present: counts, data seu_xen <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "seu_xen.rds", package = "RankMap")) seu_xen #> An object of class Seurat  #> 313 features across 150 samples within 1 assay  #> Active assay: RNA (313 features, 0 variable features) #>  2 layers present: counts, data

title: Getting Started with RankMap
text: Run cell type prediction using RankMap() function. default, RankMap uses normalized expression “data” slot. spatial datasets limited gene panels, smaller k (e.g., k = 20) typically sufficient. single-cell RNA-seq deeper coverage, larger values k (e.g., 100 200) generally recommended. result data.frame containing: cell_id, predicted_cell_type confidence
code: pred_df <- RankMap(     ref_data = seu_sc,     ref_labels = seu_sc$cell_type,     new_data = seu_xen,     k = 20 ) head(pred_df) #>   cell_id predicted_cell_type confidence #> 1    3869               Tumor     0.8829 #> 2    5257               Tumor     0.9612 #> 3    6456               Basal     0.9243 #> 4    8555                  LP     0.8847 #> 5    9243               Basal     0.9911 #> 6   10303               Basal     0.9971

title: Getting Started with RankMap
text: ground truth labels available, can evaluate prediction accuracy using:
code: perf <- EvaluatePredictionPerformance(     prediction_df = pred_df,     truth = seu_xen$cell_type_SingleR ) perf #> $overall_accuracy #> [1] 0.9466667 #>  #> $per_class_accuracy #> Basal    LP Tumor  #>  0.96  0.90  0.98  #>  #> $confusion_matrix #>        Predicted #> True    Basal LP Tumor #>   Basal    48  2     0 #>   LP        4 45     1 #>   Tumor     1  0    49

title: Getting Started with RankMap
text: Convert Seurat objects SingleCellExperiment objects:
code: library(SingleCellExperiment) #> Loading required package: SummarizedExperiment #> Loading required package: MatrixGenerics #> Loading required package: matrixStats #>  #> Attaching package: 'MatrixGenerics' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:matrixStats': #>  #>     colAlls, colAnyNAs, colAnys, colAvgsPerRowSet, colCollapse, #>     colCounts, colCummaxs, colCummins, colCumprods, colCumsums, #>     colDiffs, colIQRDiffs, colIQRs, colLogSumExps, colMadDiffs, #>     colMads, colMaxs, colMeans2, colMedians, colMins, colOrderStats, #>     colProds, colQuantiles, colRanges, colRanks, colSdDiffs, colSds, #>     colSums2, colTabulates, colVarDiffs, colVars, colWeightedMads, #>     colWeightedMeans, colWeightedMedians, colWeightedSds, #>     colWeightedVars, rowAlls, rowAnyNAs, rowAnys, rowAvgsPerColSet, #>     rowCollapse, rowCounts, rowCummaxs, rowCummins, rowCumprods, #>     rowCumsums, rowDiffs, rowIQRDiffs, rowIQRs, rowLogSumExps, #>     rowMadDiffs, rowMads, rowMaxs, rowMeans2, rowMedians, rowMins, #>     rowOrderStats, rowProds, rowQuantiles, rowRanges, rowRanks, #>     rowSdDiffs, rowSds, rowSums2, rowTabulates, rowVarDiffs, rowVars, #>     rowWeightedMads, rowWeightedMeans, rowWeightedMedians, #>     rowWeightedSds, rowWeightedVars #> Loading required package: GenomicRanges #> Loading required package: stats4 #> Loading required package: BiocGenerics #>  #> Attaching package: 'BiocGenerics' #> The following object is masked from 'package:SeuratObject': #>  #>     intersect #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     IQR, mad, sd, var, xtabs #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     anyDuplicated, aperm, append, as.data.frame, basename, cbind, #>     colnames, dirname, do.call, duplicated, eval, evalq, Filter, Find, #>     get, grep, grepl, intersect, is.unsorted, lapply, Map, mapply, #>     match, mget, order, paste, pmax, pmax.int, pmin, pmin.int, #>     Position, rank, rbind, Reduce, rownames, sapply, saveRDS, setdiff, #>     table, tapply, union, unique, unsplit, which.max, which.min #> Loading required package: S4Vectors #>  #> Attaching package: 'S4Vectors' #> The following object is masked from 'package:utils': #>  #>     findMatches #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     expand.grid, I, unname #> Loading required package: IRanges #>  #> Attaching package: 'IRanges' #> The following object is masked from 'package:sp': #>  #>     %over% #> Loading required package: GenomeInfoDb #> Loading required package: Biobase #> Welcome to Bioconductor #>  #>     Vignettes contain introductory material; view with #>     'browseVignettes()'. To cite Bioconductor, see #>     'citation("Biobase")', and for packages 'citation("pkgname")'. #>  #> Attaching package: 'Biobase' #> The following object is masked from 'package:MatrixGenerics': #>  #>     rowMedians #> The following objects are masked from 'package:matrixStats': #>  #>     anyMissing, rowMedians #>  #> Attaching package: 'SummarizedExperiment' #> The following object is masked from 'package:SeuratObject': #>  #>     Assays  sce_sc <- SingleCellExperiment(     assays = list(         counts = GetAssayData(seu_sc, layer = "counts"),         logcounts = GetAssayData(seu_sc, layer = "data")     ),     colData = seu_sc[[]] # seu_sc@meta.data )  sce_sp <- SingleCellExperiment(     assays = list(         counts = GetAssayData(seu_xen, layer = "counts"),         logcounts = GetAssayData(seu_xen, layer = "data")     ),     colData = seu_xen[[]] # seu_xen@meta.data )

title: Getting Started with RankMap
text: Run cell type prediction using RankMap() function. Set k = 100 reasonable default optimal number top-ranked genes unknown. using SummarizedExperiment input, logcounts assay used automatically.
code: pred_df <- RankMap(     ref_data = sce_sc,     ref_labels = sce_sc$cell_type,     new_data = sce_sp,     k = 100 )

title: Getting Started with RankMap
text: Compare predictions ground truth labels:
code: perf <- EvaluatePredictionPerformance(     prediction_df = pred_df,     truth = sce_sp$cell_type_SingleR ) perf #> $overall_accuracy #> [1] 0.98 #>  #> $per_class_accuracy #> Basal    LP Tumor  #>  0.98  1.00  0.96  #>  #> $confusion_matrix #>        Predicted #> True    Basal LP Tumor #>   Basal    49  1     0 #>   LP        0 50     0 #>   Tumor     2  0    48

title: Authors and Citation
text: Jinming Cheng. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Cheng J (2026). RankMap: Rank-based reference mapping fast robust cell type annotation spatial single-cell transcriptomics. R package version 0.3.0, https://github.com/jinming-cheng/RankMap.
code: @Manual{,   title = {RankMap: Rank-based reference mapping for fast and robust cell type annotation in spatial and single-cell transcriptomics},   author = {Jinming Cheng},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.3.0},   url = {https://github.com/jinming-cheng/RankMap}, }

what: Rank-based reference mapping for fast and robust cell type annotation in spatial and single-cell transcriptomics
title: Rank-based reference mapping for fast and robust cell type annotation in spatial and single-cell transcriptomics
text: RankMap fast, robust, scalable method reference-based cell type annotation single-cell spatial transcriptomics data. transforms gene expression matrices sparse ranked representations applies multinomial regression via glmnet framework predict cell types. rank-based strategy enhances robustness batch effects, platform differences, partial gene coverage, making RankMap especially suitable technologies like Xenium MERFISH. package supports flexible preprocessing, fast model training prediction, compatible Seurat, SingleCellExperiment, SpatialExperiment objects. RankMap achieves competitive accuracy significantly lower runtime existing methods SingleR, Azimuth, RCTD.

title: Rank-based reference mapping for fast and robust cell type annotation in spatial and single-cell transcriptomics
text: RankMap package can installed GitHub using:
code: devtools::install_github("jinming-cheng/RankMap")

title: Rank-based reference mapping for fast and robust cell type annotation in spatial and single-cell transcriptomics
text: example predicting cell types Xenium spatial data using well-annotated single-cell dataset reference
code: # load data seu_sc <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "seu_sc.rds",     package = "RankMap" ))  seu_xen <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "seu_xen.rds",     package = "RankMap" ))  # predict cell types, if unsure about k (default 20), set k to 100. pred_df <- RankMap(     ref_data = seu_sc,     ref_labels = seu_sc$cell_type,     new_data = seu_xen     k = 100 ) head(pred_df)

what: Compute Ranked Gene Expression Matrix — ComputeRankedMatrix
title: Compute Ranked Gene Expression Matrix — ComputeRankedMatrix
text: function transforms gene expression matrix applying top-k filtering, optional rank transformation, binning, scaling, /expression weighting.

title: Compute Ranked Gene Expression Matrix — ComputeRankedMatrix
code: ComputeRankedMatrix(   data,   weight_by_expr = TRUE,   rank_zeros = FALSE,   bin_rank = TRUE,   scale_rank = TRUE,   k = 20,   use_data = FALSE )

title: Compute Ranked Gene Expression Matrix — ComputeRankedMatrix
text: data gene expression matrix genes rows cells (spatial spots) columns. Can dense numeric matrix, sparse dgCMatrix. weight_by_expr Logical. Whether weight ranks log-transformed expression values. Default TRUE. rank_zeros Logical. Whether include zero values ranking. Default FALSE. bin_rank Logical. Whether discretize ranks bins. Default TRUE. scale_rank Logical. Whether z-score normalize ranks across columns. Default TRUE. k Integer. Number top expressed genes retain per column. Default 20. use_data Logical. TRUE, returns full input expression matrix (unfiltered unranked). Default FALSE.

title: Compute Ranked Gene Expression Matrix — ComputeRankedMatrix
text: numeric matrix ranked expression values (original expression matrix use_data = TRUE). Output always returned dense matrix.

title: Compute Ranked Gene Expression Matrix — ComputeRankedMatrix
text: use_data = TRUE, function bypasses transformation steps returns full input expression matrix. useful benchmarking performance rank-based versus raw log-normalized expression models.

title: Compute Ranked Gene Expression Matrix — ComputeRankedMatrix
code: mat <- matrix(runif(1000), nrow = 100) ranked <- ComputeRankedMatrix(mat, k = 10) raw_expr <- ComputeRankedMatrix(mat, use_data = TRUE)

what: Evaluate Prediction Accuracy and Confusion by Cell Type — EvaluatePredictionPerformance
title: Evaluate Prediction Accuracy and Confusion by Cell Type — EvaluatePredictionPerformance
text: Computes overall accuracy, per-class accuracy, confusion matrix given predicted vs. true labels.

title: Evaluate Prediction Accuracy and Confusion by Cell Type — EvaluatePredictionPerformance
code: EvaluatePredictionPerformance(   prediction_df = NULL,   truth = NULL,   low_conf_label = "unknown" )

title: Evaluate Prediction Accuracy and Confusion by Cell Type — EvaluatePredictionPerformance
text: prediction_df Output PredictRankModel (return_confidence = TRUE) filtered version. truth vector true labels matching prediction_df. low_conf_label Character. Label used low-confidence predictions (e.g., "unknown" "uncertain"). excluded evaluation. Set NULL include predictions.

title: Evaluate Prediction Accuracy and Confusion by Cell Type — EvaluatePredictionPerformance
text: list : overall_accuracy Proportion correct predictions per_class_accuracy Accuracy per true cell type confusion_matrix Contingency table (true × predicted)

title: Evaluate Prediction Accuracy and Confusion by Cell Type — EvaluatePredictionPerformance
code: # Read in single-cell reference data seu_sc <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "seu_sc.rds",     package = "RankMap" ))  # Read in Xenium spatial data seu_xen <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "seu_xen.rds",     package = "RankMap" ))  # Predict cell type for Xenium data prediction_df <- RankMap(     ref_data = seu_sc,     ref_labels = seu_sc$cell_type,     new_data = seu_xen )  performance <- EvaluatePredictionPerformance(     prediction_df = prediction_df,     truth = seu_xen$cell_type_SingleR ) performance #> $overall_accuracy #> [1] 0.9466667 #>  #> $per_class_accuracy #> Basal    LP Tumor  #>  0.96  0.90  0.98  #>  #> $confusion_matrix #>        Predicted #> True    Basal LP Tumor #>   Basal    48  2     0 #>   LP        4 45     1 #>   Tumor     1  0    49 #>

what: Extract Expression Matrix from Seurat, SummarizedExperiment, or Matrix — ExtractData
title: Extract Expression Matrix from Seurat, SummarizedExperiment, or Matrix — ExtractData
text: Extracts gene expression matrix Seurat object (default assay "data" slot), SummarizedExperiment object (assay named "logcounts"), returns input matrix already dense sparse matrix.

title: Extract Expression Matrix from Seurat, SummarizedExperiment, or Matrix — ExtractData
code: ExtractData(data)

title: Extract Expression Matrix from Seurat, SummarizedExperiment, or Matrix — ExtractData
text: data Seurat object, SummarizedExperiment object, dense numeric matrix, sparse matrix class dgCMatrix

title: Extract Expression Matrix from Seurat, SummarizedExperiment, or Matrix — ExtractData
text: numeric gene expression matrix (dense sparse), genes rows cells spots columns.

title: Extract Expression Matrix from Seurat, SummarizedExperiment, or Matrix — ExtractData
code: seu_sc <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "seu_sc.rds",     package = "RankMap" ))  # From Seurat object: mat <- ExtractData(seu_sc)

what: Order factor levels by frequency — FactorSorted
title: Order factor levels by frequency — FactorSorted
text: Takes vector, converts factor, reorders levels frequency occurrence (least frequent default).

title: Order factor levels by frequency — FactorSorted
code: FactorSorted(x, decreasing = TRUE)

title: Order factor levels by frequency — FactorSorted
text: x vector (character factor) reordered level frequency. decreasing Logical. TRUE (default), levels ordered least frequent; FALSE, least frequent.

title: Order factor levels by frequency — FactorSorted
text: factor levels ordered frequency.

title: Order factor levels by frequency — FactorSorted
text: Frequencies computed table(x), ignores NA values default. NA entries x preserved output considered level.

title: Order factor levels by frequency — FactorSorted
code: FactorSorted(c("a", "b", "a", "c", "b", "a")) #> [1] a b a c b a #> Levels: a b c FactorSorted(c("a", "b", "a", "c", "b", "a"), decreasing = FALSE) #> [1] a b a c b a #> Levels: c b a FactorSorted(factor(c("x", "y", "x", NA))) #> [1] x    y    x    <NA> #> Levels: x y

what: Tag Low-Confidence Predictions and Add Status Column — FilterLowConfidenceCells
title: Tag Low-Confidence Predictions and Add Status Column — FilterLowConfidenceCells
text: Replaces low-confidence cell type predictions placeholder label (e.g., "unknown") adds confidence status column ("confident" "uncertain").

title: Tag Low-Confidence Predictions and Add Status Column — FilterLowConfidenceCells
code: FilterLowConfidenceCells(   prediction_df,   threshold = 0.5,   low_conf_label = "unknown",   keep_confidence = TRUE )

title: Tag Low-Confidence Predictions and Add Status Column — FilterLowConfidenceCells
text: prediction_df data frame PredictRankModel (return_confidence = TRUE). threshold Numeric. Confidence threshold predictions flagged. Default 0.5. low_conf_label Character. Replacement low-confidence predictions. Default "unknown". keep_confidence Logical. Whether retain original confidence column. Default TRUE.

title: Tag Low-Confidence Predictions and Add Status Column — FilterLowConfidenceCells
text: data frame columns: cell_id, predicted_cell_type, status, nd optionally confidence.

title: Tag Low-Confidence Predictions and Add Status Column — FilterLowConfidenceCells
code: # Simulated predictions with confidence pred_df <- data.frame(     cell_id = paste0("cell", 1:5),     predicted_cell_type = c("B", "T", "B", "Myeloid", "T"),     confidence = c(0.92, 0.47, 0.88, 0.33, 0.76) )  # Apply threshold of 0.5 to flag low-confidence cells result <- FilterLowConfidenceCells(pred_df, threshold = 0.5)  # Show result print(result) #>   cell_id predicted_cell_type confidence    status #> 1   cell1                   B       0.92 confident #> 2   cell2             unknown       0.47 uncertain #> 3   cell3                   B       0.88 confident #> 4   cell4             unknown       0.33 uncertain #> 5   cell5                   T       0.76 confident  # Remove confidence column and use custom label # for low-confidence predictions result2 <- FilterLowConfidenceCells(pred_df,     threshold = 0.6,     low_conf_label = "low_conf",     keep_confidence = FALSE )

what: Mask Non-Top-K Genes in Each Cell or Spot — MaskTopKGenes
title: Mask Non-Top-K Genes in Each Cell or Spot — MaskTopKGenes
text: function zeroes top-k highly expressed genes column gene expression matrix. supports dense sparse inputs.

title: Mask Non-Top-K Genes in Each Cell or Spot — MaskTopKGenes
code: MaskTopKGenes(data, k = 20)

title: Mask Non-Top-K Genes in Each Cell or Spot — MaskTopKGenes
text: data gene expression matrix genes rows cells (spatial spots) columns. Can dense numeric matrix, sparse dgCMatrix. k Integer. Number top expressed genes retain per column. Default 20.

title: Mask Non-Top-K Genes in Each Cell or Spot — MaskTopKGenes
text: dense numeric matrix dimensions data, top-k values retained per column.

title: Mask Non-Top-K Genes in Each Cell or Spot — MaskTopKGenes
code: mat <- matrix(runif(1000), nrow = 100) masked <- MaskTopKGenes(mat, k = 10)

what: Optimize Confidence Threshold for Cell Type Prediction — OptimizeConfidenceThreshold
title: Optimize Confidence Threshold for Cell Type Prediction — OptimizeConfidenceThreshold
text: Finds confidence threshold best balances prediction accuracy retention rate. Requires ground truth labels comparison.

title: Optimize Confidence Threshold for Cell Type Prediction — OptimizeConfidenceThreshold
code: OptimizeConfidenceThreshold(   prediction_df,   truth,   thresholds = seq(0.1, 0.9, by = 0.05),   plot = TRUE )

title: Optimize Confidence Threshold for Cell Type Prediction — OptimizeConfidenceThreshold
text: prediction_df data frame PredictRankModel (return_confidence = TRUE). truth character factor vector true labels, aligned rows prediction_df. thresholds Numeric vector thresholds test. Default seq(0.1, 0.9, = 0.05). plot Logical. TRUE, generates plot accuracy vs. threshold. Default TRUE.

title: Optimize Confidence Threshold for Cell Type Prediction — OptimizeConfidenceThreshold
text: data frame summarizing accuracy retained cell count threshold.

title: Optimize Confidence Threshold for Cell Type Prediction — OptimizeConfidenceThreshold

what: Predict Cell Types, Probabilities, or Confidence Scores — PredictRankModel
title: Predict Cell Types, Probabilities, or Confidence Scores — PredictRankModel
text: Predicts cell type labels class probabilities using trained RankMap model. Supports glmnet cv.glmnet. Optionally returns data frame predicted cell types confidence scores.

title: Predict Cell Types, Probabilities, or Confidence Scores — PredictRankModel
code: PredictRankModel(   model,   new_data,   lambda = NULL,   return_probs = FALSE,   return_confidence = FALSE,   ... )

title: Predict Cell Types, Probabilities, or Confidence Scores — PredictRankModel
text: model fitted model TrainRankModel. new_data gene expression matrix genes rows cells (spatial spots) columns. Can Seurat object, SummarizedExperiment object, dense numeric matrix, sparse dgCMatrix. lambda Optional lambda value. NULL, uses lambda.min (available), else 0.01. return_probs Logical. TRUE, returns full matrix class probabilities. return_confidence Logical. TRUE, returns data frame predicted labels confidence scores. ... Additional arguments passed ComputeRankedMatrix.

title: Predict Cell Types, Probabilities, or Confidence Scores — PredictRankModel
text: character vector (default), matrix (return_probs = TRUE), data frame (return_confidence = TRUE).

title: Predict Cell Types, Probabilities, or Confidence Scores — PredictRankModel
code: # Read in single-cell reference data seu_sc <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "seu_sc.rds",     package = "RankMap" ))  # Read in Xenium spatial data seu_xen <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "seu_xen.rds",     package = "RankMap" ))  # Extract normalized expression data common_genes <- intersect(rownames(seu_sc), rownames(seu_xen)) mat <- ExtractData(seu_sc)[common_genes, ] new_mat <- ExtractData(seu_xen)[common_genes, ]  # Train a model set.seed(42) model <- TrainRankModel(mat, seu_sc$cell_type)  # Predict cell type pred <- PredictRankModel(model, new_mat)  table(predict = pred, truth = seu_xen$cell_type_SingleR) #>        truth #> predict Basal LP Tumor #>   Basal    48  4     1 #>   LP        2 45     0 #>   Tumor     0  1    49

what: RankMap: Train and Predict Cell Types Using Top-Ranked Genes — RankMap
title: RankMap: Train and Predict Cell Types Using Top-Ranked Genes — RankMap
text: unified function trains multinomial classifier reference expression data predicts cell types new dataset using top-k ranked genes. Automatically matches genes datasets, optionally performs cross-validation, returns predictions confidence scores probabilities.

title: RankMap: Train and Predict Cell Types Using Top-Ranked Genes — RankMap
code: RankMap(   ref_data = NULL,   ref_labels = NULL,   new_data = NULL,   n_feature_max = 500,   k = 20,   alpha = 0.1,   cv = FALSE,   nfolds = 5,   lambda = NULL,   return_probs = FALSE,   return_confidence = TRUE,   threshold = NULL,   return_model = FALSE,   ... )

title: RankMap: Train and Predict Cell Types Using Top-Ranked Genes — RankMap
text: ref_data Reference gene expression matrix (genes x cells), Seurat object, SummarizedExperiment object. ref_labels character factor vector cell type labels columns ref_data. new_data New data annotate. format ref_data (matrix, dgCMatrix, Seurat object SummarizedExperiment object). n_feature_max Maximum number genes use 500 genes shared. Default 500. k Number top expressed genes retain per cell (ranking). Default 20. alpha Elastic net mixing parameter glmnet. Default 0.1. cv Logical. Whether use cv.glmnet cross-validation. Default FALSE. nfolds Number folds cross-validation. Default 5. lambda Optional lambda value prediction. NULL, uses lambda.min CV defaults 0.01. return_probs Logical. TRUE, returns full class probability matrix. Default FALSE. return_confidence Logical. TRUE, returns prediction confidence score status. Default TRUE. threshold Optional numeric threshold. set return_confidence = TRUE, predictions confidence labeled "unknown". return_model Logical. TRUE, returns list containing predictions trained model. Default FALSE. ... Additional arguments passed ComputeRankedMatrix.

title: RankMap: Train and Predict Cell Types Using Top-Ranked Genes — RankMap
text: data frame predictions (default), list elements predictions model return_model = TRUE.

title: RankMap: Train and Predict Cell Types Using Top-Ranked Genes — RankMap
code: # Read in single-cell reference data seu_sc <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "seu_sc.rds",     package = "RankMap" ))  # Read in Xenium spatial data seu_xen <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "seu_xen.rds",     package = "RankMap" ))  # Predict cell type for spatial data using single-cell data as reference pred_df <- RankMap(     ref_data = seu_sc,     ref_labels = seu_sc$cell_type,     new_data = seu_xen ) head(pred_df) #>   cell_id predicted_cell_type confidence #> 1    3869               Tumor     0.8829 #> 2    5257               Tumor     0.9612 #> 3    6456               Basal     0.9243 #> 4    8555                  LP     0.8847 #> 5    9243               Basal     0.9911 #> 6   10303               Basal     0.9971

what: Sample Cells by Cell Type with Minimum Representation — SampleCellsByType
title: Sample Cells by Cell Type with Minimum Representation — SampleCellsByType
text: function samples specified total number cells metadata table, ensuring cell type represented least minimum number cells.

title: Sample Cells by Cell Type with Minimum Representation — SampleCellsByType
code: SampleCellsByType(cell_metadata, n_total_cells = 5000, min_per_type = 50)

title: Sample Cells by Cell Type with Minimum Representation — SampleCellsByType
text: cell_metadata data frame containing least two columns: cell_id cell_type. n_total_cells Total number cells sample. Default 5000. min_per_type Minimum number cells sample cell_type. Default 50.

title: Sample Cells by Cell Type with Minimum Representation — SampleCellsByType
text: data frame sampled cells least min_per_type cells per cell_type.

title: Sample Cells by Cell Type with Minimum Representation — SampleCellsByType
code: meta <- data.frame(     cell_id = paste0("cell", 1:10000),     cell_type = sample(c("T", "B", "Mac"), 10000, replace = TRUE) ) set.seed(42) sampled <- SampleCellsByType(meta,     n_total_cells = 1000,     min_per_type = 50 )

title: Train Multinomial Rank-Based Model — TrainRankModel
text: function trains multinomial logistic regression model using ranked gene expression. Optionally performs cross-validation select optimal regularization parameter.

title: Train Multinomial Rank-Based Model — TrainRankModel
code: TrainRankModel(   data = NULL,   labels = NULL,   alpha = 0.1,   cv = FALSE,   nfolds = 5,   ... )

title: Train Multinomial Rank-Based Model — TrainRankModel
text: data gene expression matrix genes rows cells (spatial spots) columns. Can Seurat object, SummarizedExperiment object, dense numeric matrix, sparse dgCMatrix. labels vector cell type labels (one per column data). alpha Elastic net mixing parameter. 1 = LASSO, 0 = Ridge. Default 0.1. cv Logical. TRUE, performs cross-validation using cv.glmnet. Default FALSE. nfolds Number folds cross-validation (used cv = TRUE). Default 5. ... Additional arguments passed ComputeRankedMatrix.

title: Train Multinomial Rank-Based Model — TrainRankModel
text: fitted glmnet cv.glmnet model object.

title: Train Multinomial Rank-Based Model — TrainRankModel
code: # Read in single-cell reference data seu_sc <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "seu_sc.rds",     package = "RankMap" ))  # Extract normalized expression data mat <- ExtractData(seu_sc)  # Train a model set.seed(42) model <- TrainRankModel(mat, seu_sc$cell_type)  # Train a model with cross-validation model <- TrainRankModel(     data = mat,     labels = seu_sc$cell_type,     cv = TRUE,     nfolds = 3 )

title: RankMap 0.99.0
text: Initial submission Bioconductor

title: RankMap 0.99.0
text: Fast, robust, scalable reference-based cell type annotation using multinomial regression sparse ranked expression matrices. Supports single-cell spatial transcriptomics data. Compatible Seurat, SingleCellExperiment, SpatialExperiment objects. Core function RankMap() provides streamlined pipeline preprocessing, model training, prediction. Customizable preprocessing: top-K gene masking, optional binning, expression weighting, scaling. ComputeRankedMatrix() – generate ranked matrices TrainRankModel() – train multinomial GLM PredictRankModel() – apply trained model query data EvaluatePredictionPerformance() – assess accuracy Optimized large datasets significantly faster runtime SingleR, Azimuth, RCTD.
